title: Machine-Readable Memory State
goal: Resolve #331 with a narrow first slice that makes Memory state more queryable without turning Memory into a database.
Classify Memory surfaces by structured-state, prose-explanation, or adapter-rendering role.
Add one compact machine-readable representation or query/report hook for a prose-first Memory surface where agents need precise answers.
non_goals: Do not remove Memory prose that is still the right explanatory layer.
Do not make Memory override planning, config, system intent, canonical docs, or explicit user instruction.
Do not build a general knowledge system, search engine, or cross-machine sync layer.
Do not absorb active task state into Memory.

intent.outcome: Agents can answer common Memory-state questions through compact structured data with provenance/confidence boundaries.
intent.constraints: Keep Memory advisory; preserve prose as explanation; avoid broad rereads and broad schema migration.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest Memory surface that benefits from structured state and add a narrow schema/query/report hook.
intent.escalation: Stop before database-like storage, repo-wide memory rewrite, or changes that reassign active planning ownership.

execution.next_step: Inspect Memory manifest/index/report paths and choose one compact structured-state slice.
execution.proof: Focused Memory/workspace tests plus summary/report proof show the structured state is queryable and planning remains clean.

invariants: Planning remains the owner of active execution state.
Structured records preserve provenance and confidence rather than flattening interpretation.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make Memory and related module state compactly queryable where agents need continuation, routing, verification, or trust answers

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Memory reports now expose a machine-readable state_model that classifies surfaces and records provenance, trust state, confidence, advisory boundary, and promotion status.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: broad Memory schema migration and database-like storage
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The existing manifest was already the right structured owner; the missing piece was a compact report projection that made the classification/query contract first-class.

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: continue planned lanes on autopilot with package dogfooding, commits, pushes, and issue closure when complete
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: first #331 slice: classify Memory surfaces and add one compact machine-readable queryable representation
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject broad Memory redesign or prose deletion that loses useful explanatory context

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ship a narrow machine-readable Memory-state slice for #331
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Memory remains advisory; no broad rereads as a success path; active planning state stays out of Memory
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: which Memory surface earns the first structured representation and which query/report hook proves it
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the slice requires broad schema migration, database storage, or changing Memory ownership boundaries

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Memory manifest/index/report/query code and tests, active planning state and execplan, package payload if source-payload checks require sync
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests packages/memory/tests -q -k "memory or report or preflight or manifest"; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before broad Memory storage redesign

context_budget.live working set: #331, Memory workflow/index/manifest, report/preflight query surfaces, active execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full memory package history and broad schema discussions
context_budget.externalize before shift: update this execplan execution_run, proof_report, and closure fields
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace summary and gh issue view #331
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: .agentic-workspace/memory/WORKFLOW.md plus the smallest relevant manifest/index files
context_budget.tiny resumability note: add one narrow structured/queryable Memory-state representation
context_budget.context-shift triggers: structured memory work becomes broader than one representation plus one proof path

stop_conditions.stop when: #331 is shipped, dogfooded, committed, pushed, and closed or residual Memory-state work is routed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: Memory would become active task state or a database-like system
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: report/preflight/query proof or planning checks fail after the structured-state slice

active_milestone.scope: Memory surface classification plus one compact structured/queryable state slice

immediate_next_action: Inspect Memory manifest/index/report code and tests.
Choose one prose-first Memory surface that agents currently need to query and add the smallest structured representation.
invariants: Structured Memory state carries provenance/confidence or explicit limits.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests packages/memory/tests -q -k "memory or report or preflight or manifest"
completion_criteria: Memory surfaces are classified as structured state, prose explanation, or adapter rendering.
At least one prose-first Memory surface has a compact machine-readable representation or a written reason to remain prose-first.
Agents can answer at least one common Memory-state question through compact data without broad rereading.
Structured records preserve provenance/confidence and advisory boundaries.
Planning and report/preflight surfaces remain clean.

execution_run.what happened: Added a machine-readable Memory state_model section to memory_report, deriving structured_state/prose_explanation/adapter_rendering classification and per-record provenance, confidence, trust state, advisory boundary, and promotion status from manifest.toml.
execution_run.scope touched: Memory report code, Memory report tests, Memory README, active planning state, and execplan closeout.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/memory/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "memory_report"; uv run ruff check packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/installer.py packages/memory/tests/test_installer.py; uv run agentic-memory-bootstrap report --target . --format json; uv run pytest tests packages/memory/tests -q -k "memory or report or preflight or manifest"; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #331 is complete and can be closed; continue with the next executable roadmap lane after archive.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, close #331, and promote the next executable lane.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; the slice stayed in Memory report/doc/test and planning closeout surfaces
finished_run_review.proof status: proved by focused and broader pytest selections, ruff, maintainer surfaces, workspace report, summary, planning checker, and diff check
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; Memory state is now classified and queryable without broad note rereads
finished_run_review.config compliance: yes; work executed from active checked-in planning state and dogfooded memory/workspace report commands
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; Memory remains advisory and prose notes remain explanation, not active task state

proof_report.validation proof: 268 targeted memory/workspace tests passed; focused memory_report tests passed; ruff passed; maintainer-surfaces passed; memory and workspace report commands produced state_model; summary and planning checker stayed clean; git diff --check passed
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: memory_report now includes state_model.kind agentic-memory/state-model/v1 with classification_counts, common_queries, record_contract, and records containing provenance/confidence/trust/advisory fields.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Make Memory and other module state more machine-readable and queryable where that improves continuation, routing, verification, and trust.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Memory surfaces are classified as structured_state/prose_explanation/adapter_rendering; all manifest notes have report records with provenance and confidence; common Memory questions such as current context, improvement candidates, and prose/adapter counts are answerable from compact JSON.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Favor compact queryable operating answers while preserving advisory memory as anti-rediscovery context.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer one structured query path over broad Memory rewrite.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can agents answer useful Memory-state questions without broad prose rereads?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #331 asked for an audit plus one narrow structured-record/query slice, not a broad Memory rewrite; the state_model report projection satisfies that acceptance.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Archived execplan plus memory_report state_model output and tests
closure_check.reopen trigger: Agents still need broad Memory prose rereads for routing, trust, freshness, promotion, or surface-classification questions after using memory_report state_model

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Memory report now exposes compact machine-readable state classification and per-record query fields over manifest-backed Memory notes.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused tests, broader targeted tests, ruff, maintainer surfaces, memory report, workspace report, summary, planning checker, and diff check passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none for #331; next executable roadmap lane is generated-tool-conformance unless #230 receives a fresh product-compression signal.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Package README documents state_model; archived plan carries proof.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted #331 from the roadmap after closing #281.
2026-04-26: Reused Memory's existing manifest as the structured owner and added a report projection instead of introducing another state file.
